page: Microsoft Azure D64a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DAv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.35 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 1638 GB of local storage. The virtualization layer is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The server exhibits poor single-core performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the bottom 10%. However, its multi-core capabilities are robust, with Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores all ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is a notable strength, with the Passmark memory score reaching the top 10% and cached small-block read bandwidth placing in the top 25%.

Given its high core count and substantial memory capacity, this server offers a balanced resource density suitable for multi-threaded workloads. While single-threaded tasks may experience performance limitations, the hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for parallelized operations, database management, and software compilation. It is well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and small-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D64a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DAv4 family, configured with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical AMD EPYC 7452 cores, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1638 GB of local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, this server delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. While single-core performance is limited, placing in the bottom 10% for stress-ng tests, its multi-core and memory capabilities are highly competitive. The server achieves top 25% rankings in Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, alongside a top 10% Passmark memory score. This resource profile makes the D64a_v4 a cost-efficient option for parallelized workloads, including database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing, where high core density and memory bandwidth are prioritized over single-threaded speed.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 1638 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core and memory performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64a_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 1638 GB of local storage, optimized for multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-density AMD EPYC virtual machine balancing sixty-four vCPUs with substantial memory and local storage for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM and 1638 GB of local storage
Delivers top-tier memory performance, ranking in the top 10% on Passmark memory benchmarks
Strong multi-core performance offset by lower single-core processing speeds
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing
